userMessage: My signal has intermittent glitches around 50ns wide on CH2. How should I set up the trigger to catch them reliably?

warnings: Detected over-concatenated SCPI command strings; split into grouped steps (max 3 per step).

response.text:
I rebuilt the empty flow to use a CH2 pulse-width window trigger around 50 ns with FastAcq enabled.

ACTIONS_JSON: {"summary":"Rebuilt the empty flow to configure a CH2 pulse-width trigger centered around 50 ns and enable FastAcq for intermittent glitch capture.","findings":["Polarity and trigger level are signal-dependent, so this flow leaves them unchanged and reads them back for review."],"suggestedFixes":["If the glitches are negative-going, change the pulse-width trigger polarity to NEGative.","Set the CH2 trigger level near the glitch mid-amplitude for best reliability.","Tighten or widen the 40 ns to 60 ns window if the actual glitch width is narrower or more variable.","Auto-repaired missing/duplicate step ids in replace_flow actions for apply compatibility.","Long semicolon command chains were split and grouped for readability (max 3 commands per step).","Filled connect/disconnect instrumentIds with workspace alias \"MSO6B\" for direct chat apply compatibility."],"actions":[{"type":"replace_flow","flow":{"name":"CH2 50 ns Glitch Trigger Setup","description":"Configures a pulse-width trigger on CH2 for intermittent glitches around 50 ns using a 40 ns to 60 ns window and FastAcq for better visibility.","backend":"pyvisa","deviceType":"SCOPE","steps":[{"type":"connect","label":"Connect","params":{"instrumentIds":["MSO6B"],"printIdn":true},"id":"s1_1"},{"id":"g_concat_iil3ha","type":"group","label":"Set A Pulse-Width Trigger","params":{},"collapsed":false,"children":[{"type":"write","label":"Set A Pulse-Width Trigger","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:MODe NORMal"},"id":"s1_2"},{"type":"write","label":"Set A Pulse-Width Trigger","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:TYPe WIDth"},"id":"cmd_5e4f5o"},{"type":"write","label":"Set A Pulse-Width Trigger","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:PULSEWidth:SOUrce CH2"},"id":"cmd_nwq65i"},{"type":"write","label":"Set A Pulse-Width Trigger","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:PULSEWidth:WHEn WIThin"},"id":"cmd_ned5cb"}]},{"type":"write","label":"Set Pulse-Width Window","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:PULSEWidth:LOWLimit 4e-8; TRIGger:A:PULSEWidth:HIGHLimit 6e-8"},"id":"s1_3"},{"type":"write","label":"Enable FastAcq","params":{"command":"ACQuire:FASTAcq:STATE ON"},"id":"s1_4"},{"type":"query","label":"Read Pulse Polarity","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:PULSEWidth:POLarity?","saveAs":"trigger_a_pulsewidth_polarity"},"id":"s1_5"},{"type":"query","label":"Read CH2 Trigger Level","params":{"command":"TRIGger:A:LEVel:CH2?","saveAs":"trigger_a_level_ch2"},"id":"s1_6"},{"type":"disconnect","label":"Disconnect","params":{"instrumentIds":["MSO6B"]},"id":"s1_7"}]}}]}

actionsJson.summary: Rebuilt the empty flow to configure a CH2 pulse-width trigger centered around 50 ns and enable FastAcq for intermittent glitch capture.
findings: Polarity and trigger level are signal-dependent, so this flow leaves them unchanged and reads them back for review.
suggestedFixes: If the glitches are negative-going, change the pulse-width trigger polarity to NEGative.
Set the CH2 trigger level near the glitch mid-amplitude for best reliability.
Tighten or widen the 40 ns to 60 ns window if the actual glitch width is narrower or more variable.
Auto-repaired missing/duplicate step ids in replace_flow actions for apply compatibility.
Long semicolon command chains were split and grouped for readability (max 3 commands per step).
Filled connect/disconnect instrumentIds with workspace alias "MSO6B" for direct chat apply compatibility.

flow.name: CH2 50 ns Glitch Trigger Setup
flow.description: Configures a pulse-width trigger on CH2 for intermittent glitches around 50 ns using a 40 ns to 60 ns window and FastAcq for better visibility.
